level_signals: Build and coach your team on best practices
Set the pace, prioritization and execution for the team
Develop the long-term vision and strategy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Product Management experience: 8+ years of relevant experience
Strong business acumen: You enjoy digging into the details of how a business works, identifying how to make it better, and building product/technical solutions to do so
Customer and Partner facing experience: You're comfortable in front of customers or third-party collaborators, and have experience navigating customer conversations
Operational savvy: You are strong operationally, capable of designing and driving a cross-functional team and process
Analytical horsepower: You dig deep into the data, think from user first principles, and deliver the right results
Crisp communicator: High attention to detail including precise and effective communications, including with external customers/partners
Results-oriented: You enjoy doing whatever it takes to execute on complex projects

scope.surface: Payments Intelligence engine and Payments Optimization products (including Radar and Authorization Boost)
primary_metrics_inferred: Payment authorization rate / successful transaction volume
core_responsibilities: Develop the long-term vision and strategy for Payments Intelligence and Optimization products, prioritize accordingly, and create an execution roadmap
Deeply understand users' needs by spending time with developers, sales, and support, and by observing patterns/trends across their feedback, the market, and data
Build and launch new capabilities and products that bring significant value to customers
Set the pace, prioritization and execution for the team
Work with a wide range of systems, processes and technologies to own and solve technical and product problems
Collaborate with stakeholders across the organization
Build and coach your team on best practices in defining product requirements and how to deliver against those requirements and the overall roadmap

green_flags: Clear product surface with named products (Radar, Authorization Boost)
High-impact domain: billions of dollars of transactions processed annually
Explicit encouragement to apply if minimum requirements are met
Preferred qualifications are labeled as a 'bonus, not a requirement', reducing gatekeeping
red_flags: No location, remote eligibility, or visa/work authorization information provided
No preferred qualifications listed despite JD structure implying they exist ('preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement') — section appears to be missing or blank
Manages people implied by coaching/team responsibilities but not explicitly stated — scope of people management is unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years + coaching/vision responsibilities; not directly stated)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'Build and coach your team' language; not explicitly stated as a people manager role)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from team mission; not stated in JD)
archetype (AI Product Manager chosen due to ML/data science centrality of Payments Intelligence; Technical PM also applicable)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location data in JD
ambiguities: JD includes a section header 'preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement' but no preferred qualifications are actually listed — either intentionally omitted or a template error.
People management scope is ambiguous: 'Build and coach your team' implies managing or mentoring PMs, but no headcount or direct report structure is stated.

Archetype could reasonably be 'Technical PM' or 'AI Product Manager' given the ML/data science focus; 'AI Product Manager' chosen due to real-time ML predictions and model-driven optimization being core to the product.
